mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over any 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5%, weak labor demand signals deteriorating consumer spending and corporate earnings, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during high-unemployment periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform baseline returns when both series rise together, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days only during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually, or the mechanism shows negative predictive power in high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and wage growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across all tested periods, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 220, rising inflation expectations force markets to price in tighter future monetary policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL surpasses 220, or inflation spikes fail to trigger equity drawdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms synchronized demand recovery, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as corporate revenue forecasts are upgraded.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and retail sales signal yields negative or flat 20-day returns during synchronized growth periods, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during periods of low unemployment indicate supply-side bottlenecks rather than demand weakness, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days due to margin compression fears.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during low unemployment periods fail to predict negative 5-day returns, or the signal performs identically across varying unemployment regimes.
